pattern: A greedy BFS-by-levels view treats each jump as expanding a reachable frontier, incrementing the jump count exactly when the current frontier is exhausted.
hints: Think of indices reachable with k jumps as a layer; the next layer is everything reachable from this one.
Track the farthest index reachable so far and the boundary of the current jump; you only need one more jump when you step past that boundary.
Sweep left to right keeping currentEnd and farthest; at each i update farthest, and when i reaches currentEnd increment jumps and move currentEnd to farthest.

edge_inputs.description: two jumps via the middle bridge
a zero that is skipped over rather than landed on
already at the end needs no jumps
single big jump clears the array
minimum steps not maximum reach each time
every step length one forces n-1 jumps
greedy must look ahead past a tempting small jump
two elements with a direct hop

stress.description: guaranteed-reachable array: each slot can always reach at least one step forward
stress.note: every non-final index jumps at least 1 so the end is always reachable
